description_long: This government building features a symmetrical, star-shaped layout with four prominent wings extending from a central circular structure. The exterior is primarily white with bold red roofing, and the central section is topped with a flagpole, indicating its official status. The building is elevated with multiple staircases and railings, and the main entrance is accentuated by a semi-circular portico with blue and white detailing. Large windows and open walkways suggest a design focused on accessibility and public use. The structure appears to be constructed from concrete and glass, with metal railings and roofing elements. The overall design is modern and institutional, suitable for administrative or civic functions.
description: A large, star-shaped government building with red roofs, white walls, and a central flagpole, featuring multiple wings and a prominent main entrance.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance with a semi-circular portico, central flagpole, and symmetrical wings extending outward.
description_view_1: This side view shows the depth of the wings and the layered structure of the central building, with the entrance partially visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the symmetry of the wings and the central circular section, with the flagpole visible on top.
description_view_3: This opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the building's depth and the arrangement of the wings, with the entrance visible from the side.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance features a wide, semi-circular portico with steps leading up to it, flanked by railings for accessibility. The entrance is visually prominent and designed to accommodate high foot traffic, with open access and clear sightlines.
